elements.text: Explore the next generation of science that takes on emerging and developing diseases of concern
Accelerate breakthrough science for the treatment and prevention of major global public health threats

Dengue: Accelerate our first-in-class, pan-serotype antiviral with novel mechanism of action to address the world’s fastest spreading mosquito-borne viral disease. Moving prophylaxis and treatment indications into clinical development. 

Tuberculosis: Accelerate the development of novel anti-TB compounds as part of Johnson & Johnson’s 10-year TB initiative, delivering a shorter, simpler next generation treatment regimen.

Leprosy: Finalize protocol and prepare to begin Phase 3 treatment trial, progressing a new indication for bedaquiline to help the fight against an ancient disease.

Coronavirus Therapeutics: Accelerate the development of novel antiviral small molecules as part of Johnson & Johnson’s commitment to fighting coronaviruses.
Anti-Microbial Resistance: Through external partnerships, progress strategy to deliver novel antibiotics that address unmet medical needs in low-to-middle income countries through exploratory investments.
Flavivirus Therapeutics: Development of broad-spectrum therapeutics addressing several flaviviruses at once.
